item.title: How to check CPU and memory utilization in Linux
item.itunes:title: How to check CPU and memory utilization in Linux
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Prerequisites
  • Check CPU and RAM usage from the terminal
  • Check CPU and RAM utilization using System Monitor
  • Check CPU and RAM utilization using Nmon Monitoring tool

Understanding and keeping tabs on your CPU and memory usage is crucial for running your system smoothly. Windows laptops have handy tools and even widgets to help you effortlessly monitor CPU, GPU, and RAM usage, but it's not as straightforward on Linux. It takes a more technical approach and lets you use a plethora of commands to monitor things like CPU and memory utilization. I've tested these methods on an Ubuntu 23.10 machine, but they work on most Linux machines.

Prerequisites

What you'll need for this process

  • A Linux-based computer
  • Access to a user account with sudo privileges
  • Access to a terminal window
  • A package installer, like apt or yum, both of which are included by default

Check CPU and RAM usage from the terminal

Using the simple top command

One of the easiest ways to get CPU and RAM usage details is by using the simple top command in the terminal. To do this:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal to open it.
  2. Enter the following command: 
    top

The terminal will respond with a list of processes that are currently running on your Linux-based system. Additionally, it'll also give you the details of user tasks, CPU load, memory usage, and more. You can press the q key on the keyboard to quit the top function before trying other commands.

If you find the frequently changing list of the task list a bit overwhelming, then you can use the following command to hide all the idle processes, making it easier to sort through the list.

top –i

​​​​​

Using the htop command for an interactive list

The top command will get the job done, but you can also install htop to get an interactive list of running processes. The only difference between the two is that top is an older command that comes pre-installed on all Linux distros, while htop needs to be installed separately and isn't standard for all Linux systems. They both provide real-time information about your system's performance, but htop offers a slightly better user experience with added colors and a more interactive user interface. To use Htop:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal.
  2. Type the following command, and enter your system password when prompted to update your list of repositories: 
    sudo apt update
    ​​​​​
  3. Install htop using apt command: 
    sudo apt install htop
  4. Run htop to get the desired CPU and RAM utilization stats. 
    Htop

You'll now see a comprehensive list of tasks, which you can scroll through. You can search for a specific task by the process name or the application by pressing the F3 key on your keyboard, or by manually selecting using a mouse click. The CPU and the RAM usage can be found towards the top of the window, above the list. You can press the F9 or the F10 button on the keyboard to kill or quit the htop command.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using System Monitor

Some Linux distributions, like Ubuntu, come with a built-in graphical monitoring tool called the System Monitor, which is similar to the Task Manager you get on Windows. It's just a simple application that you can use to monitor tasks and system usage. To use it:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal.
  2. Enter the following command: 
    gnome-system-monitor
  3. Select the Resources tab in the new window, and you'll see a complete graphical representation of CPU and RAM usage.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using Nmon Monitoring tool

A good alternative to htop

Nmon is another monitoring tool that's popular among many Linux users. IBM originally developed this system performance monitoring tool for the AIX operating system, but it was later ported to Linux. Much like htop, Nmon isn't available by default on Linux distros, meaning you'll have to install it separately. To install the Nmon monitoring tool on Ubuntu:

  1. Click the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal from the collection.
  2. Enter the following command, and also enter the password when prompted: 
    sudo apt-get install nmon
  3. Type the following command to run Nmon tool. 
    nmon
  4. Press the C or M keys on the keyboard to show CPU and memory utilization details, respectively.

Closing thoughts

There are a couple of other ways to check the CPU and memory utilization on Linux, but the ones highlighted above are among the most reliable and easiest ways. While the regular top command that's installed by default is the most popular way to check CPU and memory utilization on Linux machines, I believe alternatives like htop and Nmon tools do a better job of providing the info with a more interactive interface. Alternatively, you can also install and use Bpytop, which is essentially a Python implementation of htop that requires the Python package manager pip to work.

  3. Install htop using apt command: 
    sudo apt install htop
  4. Run htop to get the desired CPU and RAM utilization stats. 
    Htop

You'll now see a comprehensive list of tasks, which you can scroll through. You can search for a specific task by the process name or the application by pressing the F3 key on your keyboard, or by manually selecting using a mouse click. The CPU and the RAM usage can be found towards the top of the window, above the list. You can press the F9 or the F10 button on the keyboard to kill or quit the htop command.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using System Monitor

Some Linux distributions, like Ubuntu, come with a built-in graphical monitoring tool called the System Monitor, which is similar to the Task Manager you get on Windows. It's just a simple application that you can use to monitor tasks and system usage. To use it:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal.
  2. Enter the following command: 
    gnome-system-monitor
  3. Select the Resources tab in the new window, and you'll see a complete graphical representation of CPU and RAM usage.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using Nmon Monitoring tool

A good alternative to htop

Nmon is another monitoring tool that's popular among many Linux users. IBM originally developed this system performance monitoring tool for the AIX operating system, but it was later ported to Linux. Much like htop, Nmon isn't available by default on Linux distros, meaning you'll have to install it separately. To install the Nmon monitoring tool on Ubuntu:

  1. Click the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal from the collection.
  2. Enter the following command, and also enter the password when prompted: 
    sudo apt-get install nmon
  3. Type the following command to run Nmon tool. 
    nmon
  4. Press the C or M keys on the keyboard to show CPU and memory utilization details, respectively.

Closing thoughts

There are a couple of other ways to check the CPU and memory utilization on Linux, but the ones highlighted above are among the most reliable and easiest ways. While the regular top command that's installed by default is the most popular way to check CPU and memory utilization on Linux machines, I believe alternatives like htop and Nmon tools do a better job of providing the info with a more interactive interface. Alternatively, you can also install and use Bpytop, which is essentially a Python implementation of htop that requires the Python package manager pip to work.
item.content:encoded:
Quick Links

  • Prerequisites
  • Check CPU and RAM usage from the terminal
  • Check CPU and RAM utilization using System Monitor
  • Check CPU and RAM utilization using Nmon Monitoring tool

Understanding and keeping tabs on your CPU and memory usage is crucial for running your system smoothly. Windows laptops have handy tools and even widgets to help you effortlessly monitor CPU, GPU, and RAM usage, but it's not as straightforward on Linux. It takes a more technical approach and lets you use a plethora of commands to monitor things like CPU and memory utilization. I've tested these methods on an Ubuntu 23.10 machine, but they work on most Linux machines.

Prerequisites

What you'll need for this process

  • A Linux-based computer
  • Access to a user account with sudo privileges
  • Access to a terminal window
  • A package installer, like apt or yum, both of which are included by default

Check CPU and RAM usage from the terminal

Using the simple top command

One of the easiest ways to get CPU and RAM usage details is by using the simple top command in the terminal. To do this:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal to open it.
  2. Enter the following command: 
    top

The terminal will respond with a list of processes that are currently running on your Linux-based system. Additionally, it'll also give you the details of user tasks, CPU load, memory usage, and more. You can press the q key on the keyboard to quit the top function before trying other commands.

If you find the frequently changing list of the task list a bit overwhelming, then you can use the following command to hide all the idle processes, making it easier to sort through the list.

top –i

​​​​​

Using the htop command for an interactive list

The top command will get the job done, but you can also install htop to get an interactive list of running processes. The only difference between the two is that top is an older command that comes pre-installed on all Linux distros, while htop needs to be installed separately and isn't standard for all Linux systems. They both provide real-time information about your system's performance, but htop offers a slightly better user experience with added colors and a more interactive user interface. To use Htop:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal.
  2. Type the following command, and enter your system password when prompted to update your list of repositories: 
    sudo apt update
    ​​​​​
  3. Install htop using apt command: 
    sudo apt install htop
  4. Run htop to get the desired CPU and RAM utilization stats. 
    Htop

You'll now see a comprehensive list of tasks, which you can scroll through. You can search for a specific task by the process name or the application by pressing the F3 key on your keyboard, or by manually selecting using a mouse click. The CPU and the RAM usage can be found towards the top of the window, above the list. You can press the F9 or the F10 button on the keyboard to kill or quit the htop command.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using System Monitor

Some Linux distributions, like Ubuntu, come with a built-in graphical monitoring tool called the System Monitor, which is similar to the Task Manager you get on Windows. It's just a simple application that you can use to monitor tasks and system usage. To use it:

  1. Click on the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal.
  2. Enter the following command: 
    gnome-system-monitor
  3. Select the Resources tab in the new window, and you'll see a complete graphical representation of CPU and RAM usage.

Check CPU and RAM utilization using Nmon Monitoring tool

A good alternative to htop

Nmon is another monitoring tool that's popular among many Linux users. IBM originally developed this system performance monitoring tool for the AIX operating system, but it was later ported to Linux. Much like htop, Nmon isn't available by default on Linux distros, meaning you'll have to install it separately. To install the Nmon monitoring tool on Ubuntu:

  1. Click the Show apps button on your system's main screen, and select Terminal from the collection.
  2. Enter the following command, and also enter the password when prompted: 
    sudo apt-get install nmon
  3. Type the following command to run Nmon tool. 
    nmon
  4. Press the C or M keys on the keyboard to show CPU and memory utilization details, respectively.

Closing thoughts

There are a couple of other ways to check the CPU and memory utilization on Linux, but the ones highlighted above are among the most reliable and easiest ways. While the regular top command that's installed by default is the most popular way to check CPU and memory utilization on Linux machines, I believe alternatives like htop and Nmon tools do a better job of providing the info with a more interactive interface. Alternatively, you can also install and use Bpytop, which is essentially a Python implementation of htop that requires the Python package manager pip to work.

item.title: Best deals on Samsung Galaxy S23 series in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best deals on Samsung Galaxy S23 series in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Although we're on the cusp of new smartphones making their debuts in 2024, Samsung's Galaxy S23 series devices are still some of the best Android smartphones you can buy right now. Despite an early release in 2023, the Galaxy S23 Ultra has dominated the market and positioned itself at the top of many "best of" lists across the internet thanks to its refined design, powerful processor, and excellent cameras.

The Galaxy S23+ and S23 also took top honors in many categories, solidifying Galaxy smartphones as some of the best you can buy, no matter your preference. Of course, these handsets aren't cheap, with the base model Galaxy S23 starting at $800, and the Galaxy S23 Ultra starting at $1200. It's always better to get a deal, so that's why we've compiled some of the best promotions available on Samsung Galaxy S23 series devices. With that said, let's dive into these stellar savings.

Galaxy S23

The Samsung Galaxy S23 is the most compact phone in the lineup, but that doesn't mean it's lacking when it comes to its specifications. The Galaxy S23 is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor with 8GB RAM, with two internal storage options coming in at 128GB and 256GB. It has a 6.1-inch AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits, and a variable refresh rate of 48 to 120Hz.

When it comes to cameras, the Galaxy S23 has a 10MP selfie camera, and triple camera setup on the rear, with a 50MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, and 10MP telephoto. You'll be able to go a full day with moderate use thanks to the phone's 3900mAh capacity battery. As far as colors go, you're going to get four standard choices, with Phantom Black, Cream, Green, and Lavender. As far as pricing goes, you're looking at $799.99 for the 128GB model, and $859.99 for the 256GB version.

Amazon

Amazon is one of the best places that you can buy the Samsung Galaxy S23. The online retailer is known for its great prices and fast delivery times and offers the Galaxy S23 for $100 off its retail price. The retailer does carry both the 128GB and 256GB versions of the handset, but it looks like, at the moment, it only has the 128GB model in stock.

In addition to the fantastic price, you can also take advantage of special financing offers with up to 12 months, no interest, when using the retailer's own credit card. Furthermore, although the phone does come with a one-year warranty from the manufacturer, you can still purchase additional coverage that will cover accidental damage for up to three years.

Amazon
$700 $800 Save $100

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23 from Amazon in four different colors. While the retailer does carry the 128GB and 256GB versions, as of this time, it currently only has the former model available. Luckily, you can get a sweet discount that knocks $100 off, and it's available in four different colors.

$700 at Amazon

AT&T

If you're thinking about signing up with a wireless carrier, and you need a new phone, AT&T might be the way to go. The wireless carrier isn't offering any direct discounts on the Galaxy S23 right now, but it is offering a hefty discount on the handset if you're willing to trade in an existing device. With an eligible handset, you can earn up to an $800 credit, which will make the Galaxy S23 free.

While this is a nice promotion, there is one thing to know, and that is that the $800 credit won't be issued all at once. AT&T will issue the credit monthly over a 36-month period, which means if you want the full $800 credit, you're going to need to stick with the wireless carrier for three years. This isn't a bad thing, but just make sure that the area where you use the phone has good cellular coverage.

AT&T
$800 off with trade in

The Samsung Galaxy S23 is available from AT&T and while the carrier isn't offering any direct discount on the device, it can be had for free with trade-in. For a limited time, when you trade in an eligible device, you can earn up to $800 in credit. This credit will be divided over a 36-month period, which means you'll be locked in to AT&T for three years.

128GB model

T-Mobile

T-Mobile is offering a great deal on the Galaxy S23, which drops the price of the Galaxy S23 to free, if you're willing to sign up for a Go5G Plus line and trade in a device. The wireless carrier will issue an $800 credit, which will be split up into monthly credits over a 24-month period. T-Mobile offers the 128GB version of the Galaxy S23 in Phantom Black and Lavender. So, if the AT&T deal isn't quite what you're looking for, then maybe this deal from T-Mobile will be a better fit for you.

T-Mobile
$800 off with trade in

T-Mobile has the Galaxy S23 available and while it doesn't offer any direct discounts on the device, it can be had for free with eligible trade-in. By signing up and trading in, you can earn up to $800 in credit. This credit will be divided over a 24-month period.

128GB model

Verizon

Out of all the major wireless carriers, Verizon is offering the best deal on the Galaxy S23. While the wireless carrier does have the same stipulation as AT&T and T-Mobile, requiring a trade in to get a fantastic discount, the big difference here is that Verizon is accepting any device for trade in.

Just like the other wireless carriers, you won't be getting the $800 credit upfront, and the company will be issuing the credit on a monthly basis over 36 months. In addition, those that are already with Verizon can still take advantage of this deal, with upgrades also being accepted as well.

Furthermore, the wireless carrier is also offering more deals, like $350 off the Galaxy Watch6 or Watch6 Classic with the purchase of the Galaxy S23. When it comes to colors, Verizon customers will have the choice of choosing between Phantom Black, Cream, Green, and Lavender. Like other carriers, it currently only has the 128GB model in stock.

Verizon

Verizon has the Galaxy S23 available and while the wireless carrier doesn't offer any direct discounts, you can get it for free by trading in any wireless device and signing up for service. Verizon will offer $800 in credit, which will be issued over 36 months. Furthermore, the carrier will also provide up to $350 towards the purchase of a Galaxy Watch 6 or Galaxy Watch 6 Classic.

See at Verizon

Galaxy S23+

The Samsung Galaxy S23+ is the middle child of the lineup, offering a slightly larger screen than the Galaxy S23, coming in at 6.6 inches. Of course, you still get the same beautiful display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits, and a variable refresh rate from 48 to 120Hz. The handset is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor paired with 8GB RAM, and there's a choice of two different internal storage options, coming in at 256GB and 512GB.

When it comes to cameras, you get the same great setup as the Galaxy S23, with the Galaxy S23+ having a 10MP selfie camera, and a 50MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, and 10MP telephoto on the rear. Since this is a slightly larger phone, you're also going to get a slightly larger battery, with capacity coming in at 4700mAh, which is more than enough for a full day with moderate use. With pricing, the 256GB model comes in at $999.99, and the 512GB model comes in at $1119.99.

Amazon

Amazon is one of the best places that you can buy the Samsung Galaxy S23+, especially at this time, because other retailers are not offering a discount on the product. Amazon has the 256GB version for $987.01, and the 512GB variant priced at $1010. Just be aware that there are different colors available, and from what we've seen, there are some fluctuations in prices depending on which one you choose.

While not the largest savings we've seen to date, it easily beats competitors like Samsung and Best Buy. Best of all, if you're an Amazon credit card holder, you can take advantage of special financing offers for up to 12 months, and you can also purchase additional warranty on the phone as well, with up to three years of coverage against accidental damage.

Amazon
$987 $1000 Save $13

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23+ from Amazon in four different colors. While the online retailer is offering discounts on the 256GB and 512GB models, pricing isn't quite stable, with different colors getting different discounts. Be sure to check your configuration to see how much you'll save.

256GB model 512GB model

AT&T

Although AT&T doesn't offer the Galaxy S23+ directly, but it can still be purchased with the wireless carrier through Best Buy. So if you've been thinking about signing up for new service, or you just want to upgrade your line, you're in for a huge treat, because Best Buy has a phenomenal deal on the Galaxy S23+.

If you're signing up for a new line of service with AT&T, you can now save $400 off on the 256GB version of the Galaxy S23+. Those upgrading can take advantage too, with the retailer offering $300 off for a limited time. If you want, you'll have the option to purchase at this discounted rate, or you can make monthly payments on the device over 36 months.

The retailer does have trade in promotions as well, offering up to $1000 in credit towards the purchase of a new phone. Furthermore, if you're a Best Buy credit card holder, you can also take advantage of financing offers too. This is one of the best deals you'll find on the Galaxy S23+ if you're looking to sign up with AT&T.

AT&T
$600 $1000 Save $400

You can now purchase the Samsung Galaxy S23+ from AT&T through Best Buy. The retailer is offering a direct discount on the device, slashing $400 off for activating a new line, and $300 off when upgrading a current one. Customers can also take advantage of trade-in deals and financing using carrier payments or the Best Buy credit card.

256GB model

Verizon

Whether you're a current Verizon customer looking to upgrade, or just want to sign up for a new line, going through Best Buy to get a new Galaxy S23+ is going to be the best option for you. Best Buy has the Phantom Black 256GB model in stock and is offering $300 off for a limited time. While you can purchase this device for that price outright, you will also have the option of financing the handset through the carrier or using the Best Buy credit card. The retailer is also accepting trade-ins, so if you have an older device, you can potentially get up to a $1000 credit if your device is eligible.

Verizon
$700 $1000 Save $300

The Samsung Galaxy S23+ is available for Verizon through Best Buy. The retailer is offering a direct discount on the device, taking $300 off the retail price. Customers can also take advantage of trade-in deals with up to $1000 credit being offered for eligible handsets. Furthermore, there are financing options using carrier payments or the Best Buy credit card.

256GB model

Galaxy S23 Ultra

If you're looking for the best, the Galaxy S23 Ultra is going to be it. The Galaxy S23 Ultra is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor with either 8GB or 12GB RAM. If you opt for the 8GB RAM model, you're going to get 256GB of internal storage. The 12GB RAM model comes in two configurations, one with 512GB and the other with 1TB.

When it comes to the screen, you're going to get a large 6.8-inch AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits. The display also has a curved design and a variable refresh rate that can range from 1 to 120Hz. As far as the cameras, the Galaxy S23 Ultra offers the most advanced setup out of the trio, with a 200MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, two 10MP telephoto, and a 12MP selfie camera.

Battery life will last you all-day with moderate use thanks to the phone's 5000mAh capacity, and you'll also be able to get more creative thanks to the built-in S Pen stylus. When it comes to pricing, the 256GB model comes in at $1199.99, the 512GB version at $1379.99, and the 1TB model at $1619.99.

Amazon

Although Amazon does offer many discounted Galaxy S23 Ultra models, the online retailer does not currently sell any models directly. These models offered are through third-party sellers, which means, you're going to have to be a little more careful when making your purchase. While there are many third-party vendors on the site, you'll want to stick with one that offers shipment through Amazon or offers a proper return policy.

As far as discounts go, they vary widely, but the model we found is now just $907.50 for the 256GB model, which is a great deal. Since this is Amazon, you're going to get excellent shipping speeds, especially if you're a Prime member, and you'll be able to take advantage of special financing offers if you use an Amazon credit card. Furthermore, you'll also be able to purchase extended coverage for up to three years.

Amazon
$908 $1200 Save $292

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra from Amazon but it will be through third-party retailers. While the discounts will vary, you want to make sure that you're purchasing from a trusted seller, one that offers a sturdy return policy and warranty on the product.

256GB model

AT&T

AT&T does not offer the Galaxy S23 Ultra directly, but you can sign up for service or upgrade your line through Best Buy and get a sweet discount in the process. When it comes to pricing, there are a few different options with the retailer offering the 256GB and 512GB models, but the discount will be the same no matter which one you choose.

If you're signing up for a new line of service, you can score $450 off the retail price of the phone, and if you're thinking about upgrading, that's also an option, and you can save $350. Now, if you're going with the 512GB model, the discount will come in at $929.99 with a new line, and $1029.99 with an upgrade. For the 256GB model, the price will come down to $749.99 with a new line, and $849.99 with an upgrade.

While you can pay these amounts up front, you'll also have the option to finance the phone through AT&T and make monthly payments instead. If you have a Best Buy credit card, you can also use that to finance your purchase as well. In addition to the savings above, Best Buy is also offering up to $1000 in trade in credit when turning in an eligible product.

AT&T
$750 $1200 Save $450

You can save up to $450 on the Galaxy S23 Ultra when signing up for service with AT&T through Best Buy. The retailer also has financing available and trade-in offers worth up to $1000.

256GB model

Verizon

If you're looking to sign up for a new line of service, going through Best Buy to get a new Galaxy S23 Ultra is going to be the best option, with the retailer offering $350 off the 256GB model for a limited time. When it comes to payment options, you can pay the full amount up front, or you can finance it through the carrier. If you're a Best Buy cardholder, you can also use the card to finance the total amount as well. In addition, the retailer also offers trade in credit worth up to $1000 if you have an eligible device.

Verizon
$850 $1200 Save $350

You can save $300 on the Galaxy S23 Ultra when signing up for service with Verizon through Best Buy. The retailer also has financing available and trade-in offers worth up to $1000.

$850 at Verizon

These are some of the best deals you're going to find on Samsung Galaxy S23 series devices. Whether you're buying from a retailer or wireless carrier, you're going to have some great options to choose from, saving you hundreds in the process. Of course, if you're intending to keep your smartphone for a while, be sure to buy a case or maybe grab an accessory for your Galaxy S23 to enhance the experience.

While you can purchase this device for that price outright, you will also have the option of financing the handset through the carrier or using the Best Buy credit card. The retailer is also accepting trade-ins, so if you have an older device, you can potentially get up to a $1000 credit if your device is eligible.

Verizon
$700 $1000 Save $300

The Samsung Galaxy S23+ is available for Verizon through Best Buy. The retailer is offering a direct discount on the device, taking $300 off the retail price. Customers can also take advantage of trade-in deals with up to $1000 credit being offered for eligible handsets. Furthermore, there are financing options using carrier payments or the Best Buy credit card.

256GB model

Galaxy S23 Ultra

If you're looking for the best, the Galaxy S23 Ultra is going to be it. The Galaxy S23 Ultra is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor with either 8GB or 12GB RAM. If you opt for the 8GB RAM model, you're going to get 256GB of internal storage. The 12GB RAM model comes in two configurations, one with 512GB and the other with 1TB.

When it comes to the screen, you're going to get a large 6.8-inch AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits. The display also has a curved design and a variable refresh rate that can range from 1 to 120Hz. As far as the cameras, the Galaxy S23 Ultra offers the most advanced setup out of the trio, with a 200MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, two 10MP telephoto, and a 12MP selfie camera.

Battery life will last you all-day with moderate use thanks to the phone's 5000mAh capacity, and you'll also be able to get more creative thanks to the built-in S Pen stylus. When it comes to pricing, the 256GB model comes in at $1199.99, the 512GB version at $1379.99, and the 1TB model at $1619.99.

Amazon

Although Amazon does offer many discounted Galaxy S23 Ultra models, the online retailer does not currently sell any models directly. These models offered are through third-party sellers, which means, you're going to have to be a little more careful when making your purchase. While there are many third-party vendors on the site, you'll want to stick with one that offers shipment through Amazon or offers a proper return policy.

As far as discounts go, they vary widely, but the model we found is now just $907.50 for the 256GB model, which is a great deal. Since this is Amazon, you're going to get excellent shipping speeds, especially if you're a Prime member, and you'll be able to take advantage of special financing offers if you use an Amazon credit card. Furthermore, you'll also be able to purchase extended coverage for up to three years.

Amazon
$908 $1200 Save $292

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra from Amazon but it will be through third-party retailers. While the discounts will vary, you want to make sure that you're purchasing from a trusted seller, one that offers a sturdy return policy and warranty on the product.

256GB model

AT&T

AT&T does not offer the Galaxy S23 Ultra directly, but you can sign up for service or upgrade your line through Best Buy and get a sweet discount in the process. When it comes to pricing, there are a few different options with the retailer offering the 256GB and 512GB models, but the discount will be the same no matter which one you choose.

If you're signing up for a new line of service, you can score $450 off the retail price of the phone, and if you're thinking about upgrading, that's also an option, and you can save $350. Now, if you're going with the 512GB model, the discount will come in at $929.99 with a new line, and $1029.99 with an upgrade. For the 256GB model, the price will come down to $749.99 with a new line, and $849.99 with an upgrade.

While you can pay these amounts up front, you'll also have the option to finance the phone through AT&T and make monthly payments instead. If you have a Best Buy credit card, you can also use that to finance your purchase as well. In addition to the savings above, Best Buy is also offering up to $1000 in trade in credit when turning in an eligible product.

AT&T
$750 $1200 Save $450

You can save up to $450 on the Galaxy S23 Ultra when signing up for service with AT&T through Best Buy. The retailer also has financing available and trade-in offers worth up to $1000.

256GB model

Verizon

If you're looking to sign up for a new line of service, going through Best Buy to get a new Galaxy S23 Ultra is going to be the best option, with the retailer offering $350 off the 256GB model for a limited time. When it comes to payment options, you can pay the full amount up front, or you can finance it through the carrier. If you're a Best Buy cardholder, you can also use the card to finance the total amount as well. In addition, the retailer also offers trade in credit worth up to $1000 if you have an eligible device.

Verizon
$850 $1200 Save $350

You can save $300 on the Galaxy S23 Ultra when signing up for service with Verizon through Best Buy. The retailer also has financing available and trade-in offers worth up to $1000.

$850 at Verizon

These are some of the best deals you're going to find on Samsung Galaxy S23 series devices. Whether you're buying from a retailer or wireless carrier, you're going to have some great options to choose from, saving you hundreds in the process. Of course, if you're intending to keep your smartphone for a while, be sure to buy a case or maybe grab an accessory for your Galaxy S23 to enhance the experience.
item.content:encoded:
Although we're on the cusp of new smartphones making their debuts in 2024, Samsung's Galaxy S23 series devices are still some of the best Android smartphones you can buy right now. Despite an early release in 2023, the Galaxy S23 Ultra has dominated the market and positioned itself at the top of many "best of" lists across the internet thanks to its refined design, powerful processor, and excellent cameras.

The Galaxy S23+ and S23 also took top honors in many categories, solidifying Galaxy smartphones as some of the best you can buy, no matter your preference. Of course, these handsets aren't cheap, with the base model Galaxy S23 starting at $800, and the Galaxy S23 Ultra starting at $1200. It's always better to get a deal, so that's why we've compiled some of the best promotions available on Samsung Galaxy S23 series devices. With that said, let's dive into these stellar savings.

Galaxy S23

The Samsung Galaxy S23 is the most compact phone in the lineup, but that doesn't mean it's lacking when it comes to its specifications. The Galaxy S23 is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor with 8GB RAM, with two internal storage options coming in at 128GB and 256GB. It has a 6.1-inch AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits, and a variable refresh rate of 48 to 120Hz.

When it comes to cameras, the Galaxy S23 has a 10MP selfie camera, and triple camera setup on the rear, with a 50MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, and 10MP telephoto. You'll be able to go a full day with moderate use thanks to the phone's 3900mAh capacity battery. As far as colors go, you're going to get four standard choices, with Phantom Black, Cream, Green, and Lavender. As far as pricing goes, you're looking at $799.99 for the 128GB model, and $859.99 for the 256GB version.

Amazon

Amazon is one of the best places that you can buy the Samsung Galaxy S23. The online retailer is known for its great prices and fast delivery times and offers the Galaxy S23 for $100 off its retail price. The retailer does carry both the 128GB and 256GB versions of the handset, but it looks like, at the moment, it only has the 128GB model in stock.

In addition to the fantastic price, you can also take advantage of special financing offers with up to 12 months, no interest, when using the retailer's own credit card. Furthermore, although the phone does come with a one-year warranty from the manufacturer, you can still purchase additional coverage that will cover accidental damage for up to three years.

Amazon
$700 $800 Save $100

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23 from Amazon in four different colors. While the retailer does carry the 128GB and 256GB versions, as of this time, it currently only has the former model available. Luckily, you can get a sweet discount that knocks $100 off, and it's available in four different colors.

$700 at Amazon

AT&T

If you're thinking about signing up with a wireless carrier, and you need a new phone, AT&T might be the way to go. The wireless carrier isn't offering any direct discounts on the Galaxy S23 right now, but it is offering a hefty discount on the handset if you're willing to trade in an existing device. With an eligible handset, you can earn up to an $800 credit, which will make the Galaxy S23 free.

While this is a nice promotion, there is one thing to know, and that is that the $800 credit won't be issued all at once. AT&T will issue the credit monthly over a 36-month period, which means if you want the full $800 credit, you're going to need to stick with the wireless carrier for three years. This isn't a bad thing, but just make sure that the area where you use the phone has good cellular coverage.

AT&T
$800 off with trade in

The Samsung Galaxy S23 is available from AT&T and while the carrier isn't offering any direct discount on the device, it can be had for free with trade-in. For a limited time, when you trade in an eligible device, you can earn up to $800 in credit. This credit will be divided over a 36-month period, which means you'll be locked in to AT&T for three years.

128GB model

T-Mobile

T-Mobile is offering a great deal on the Galaxy S23, which drops the price of the Galaxy S23 to free, if you're willing to sign up for a Go5G Plus line and trade in a device. The wireless carrier will issue an $800 credit, which will be split up into monthly credits over a 24-month period. T-Mobile offers the 128GB version of the Galaxy S23 in Phantom Black and Lavender. So, if the AT&T deal isn't quite what you're looking for, then maybe this deal from T-Mobile will be a better fit for you.

T-Mobile
$800 off with trade in

T-Mobile has the Galaxy S23 available and while it doesn't offer any direct discounts on the device, it can be had for free with eligible trade-in. By signing up and trading in, you can earn up to $800 in credit. This credit will be divided over a 24-month period.

128GB model

Verizon

Out of all the major wireless carriers, Verizon is offering the best deal on the Galaxy S23. While the wireless carrier does have the same stipulation as AT&T and T-Mobile, requiring a trade in to get a fantastic discount, the big difference here is that Verizon is accepting any device for trade in.

Just like the other wireless carriers, you won't be getting the $800 credit upfront, and the company will be issuing the credit on a monthly basis over 36 months. In addition, those that are already with Verizon can still take advantage of this deal, with upgrades also being accepted as well.

Furthermore, the wireless carrier is also offering more deals, like $350 off the Galaxy Watch6 or Watch6 Classic with the purchase of the Galaxy S23. When it comes to colors, Verizon customers will have the choice of choosing between Phantom Black, Cream, Green, and Lavender. Like other carriers, it currently only has the 128GB model in stock.

Verizon

Verizon has the Galaxy S23 available and while the wireless carrier doesn't offer any direct discounts, you can get it for free by trading in any wireless device and signing up for service. Verizon will offer $800 in credit, which will be issued over 36 months. Furthermore, the carrier will also provide up to $350 towards the purchase of a Galaxy Watch 6 or Galaxy Watch 6 Classic.

See at Verizon

Galaxy S23+

The Samsung Galaxy S23+ is the middle child of the lineup, offering a slightly larger screen than the Galaxy S23, coming in at 6.6 inches. Of course, you still get the same beautiful display with a peak brightness of 1750 nits, and a variable refresh rate from 48 to 120Hz. The handset is powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor paired with 8GB RAM, and there's a choice of two different internal storage options, coming in at 256GB and 512GB.

When it comes to cameras, you get the same great setup as the Galaxy S23, with the Galaxy S23+ having a 10MP selfie camera, and a 50MP main camera, 12MP ultrawide, and 10MP telephoto on the rear. Since this is a slightly larger phone, you're also going to get a slightly larger battery, with capacity coming in at 4700mAh, which is more than enough for a full day with moderate use. With pricing, the 256GB model comes in at $999.99, and the 512GB model comes in at $1119.99.

Amazon

Amazon is one of the best places that you can buy the Samsung Galaxy S23+, especially at this time, because other retailers are not offering a discount on the product. Amazon has the 256GB version for $987.01, and the 512GB variant priced at $1010. Just be aware that there are different colors available, and from what we've seen, there are some fluctuations in prices depending on which one you choose.

While not the largest savings we've seen to date, it easily beats competitors like Samsung and Best Buy. Best of all, if you're an Amazon credit card holder, you can take advantage of special financing offers for up to 12 months, and you can also purchase additional warranty on the phone as well, with up to three years of coverage against accidental damage.

Amazon
$987 $1000 Save $13

You can now grab the Samsung Galaxy S23+ from Amazon in four different colors. While the online retailer is offering discounts on the 256GB and 512GB models, pricing isn't quite stable, with different colors getting different discounts. Be sure to check your configuration to see how much you'll save.

256GB model 512GB model

AT&T

Although AT&T doesn't offer the Galaxy S23+ directly, but it can still be purchased with the wireless carrier through Best Buy. So if you've been thinking about signing up for new service, or you just want to upgrade your line, you're in for a huge treat, because Best Buy has a phenomenal deal on the Galaxy S23+.

If you're signing up for a new line of service with AT&T, you can now save $400 off on the 256GB version of the Galaxy S23+. Those upgrading can take advantage too, with the retailer offering $300 off for a limited time. If you want, you'll have the option to purchase at this discounted rate, or you can make monthly payments on the device over 36 months.

The retailer does have trade in promotions as well, offering up to $1000 in credit towards the purchase of a new phone. Furthermore, if you're a Best Buy credit card holder, you can also take advantage of financing offers too. This is one of the best deals you'll find on the Galaxy S23+ if you're looking to sign up with AT&T.

AT&T
$600 $1000 Save $400

You can now purchase the Samsung Galaxy S23+ from AT&T through Best Buy. The retailer is offering a direct discount on the device, slashing $400 off for activating a new line, and $300 off when upgrading a current one. Customers can also take advantage of trade-in deals and financing using carrier payments or the Best Buy credit card.

256GB model
